and decisions in regard to these
combinations. The president scoli-
ed congress sharply for their fail
ure to attack the subject of the

lation on this subject as occasion

He declared the issuance of Sc

BOSTON, April 2S-In making
reference to scandal in collision in
bidding by certain contractors on
supplies in Boston the president
stirred up. a hornets nest. Con-
tractors declare that he has done
them injustice. President Smith, of
the Smjth-Lowell Co., denied that
his company has been made to act
as a scapegoat for the higher ups
Other contractors also ienounce
the president for using their
names.


The president in supporting his
argument for trust regulation sub
mitted extracts from the report of
Nathan Mathews to the Boston
council showing that the American
Bridge Co. and others had entered
into an agreement that practically
eliminated bidding on steel work in
New England and made all such
transactions fraudulent.


The house could not consider the
message on account of the red tape
now governing that body, and the
senate postponed consideration OF
the matter until today. 1t is UNI
likely that any change will be eff
fected, as congress has mapped out
the program of just what it does
not intend to do and will in all
probability follow it to the adjourn
ment.


The president states that the en-
ployers' liability bin recently pass
ed falls short of what it should be,
but regards 1t as a step forward.
He hopes it will be the stepping
stone for legislation guaranteeing
reimbursement for injuries of all
classes Of employees in public
service.


The president rehearses his
previous requests for legislation
he has championed, among them
being the postals saving banks and
financial relief He renews and
emphasizes his recommendations
for measures on behalf of labor,
and warns congress against closing
it's ears to the demand of public
opinion for restrictions on the
power of injunction. The same at
titude is taken on government con-
troll of COrpOratiOns.
